deck.title: HTML Lane Benchmark
deck.audience: Skill builders evaluating whether Knowledge Cat can produce a benchmark-grade HTML deck.
deck.outcome: Show that the HTML lane can combine story discipline, registered layouts, visual-system control, local image slots, and QA artifacts.
deck.main_promise: A browser deck becomes benchmark-grade when narrative, visual system, layout registry, and screenshot QA are locked together.
deck.narrative_spine: Tension -> mechanism -> proof -> operating standard -> next action
deck.format: Single-folder browser deck with screenshot QA artifacts
assumptions: This is a local benchmark fixture, not a client deliverable.
The image asset is a synthetic local SVG used to test image-slot handling.

slides.action_title: HTML needs a production lock before it can become a reliable deck.
slides.key_message: The HTML lane should feel like a controlled presentation product, not a freeform web page.
slides.visual: Sparse architectural hero with top navigation and one large claim.

slides.action_title: The enemy is not bad CSS; it is unbounded layout freedom.
slides.key_message: Without registered layouts, a browser deck drifts slide by slide.
evidence: Benchmark synthesis from Guizang and Frontend Slides mechanisms
slides.visual: Dark typographic reset with a thin grid.

slides.action_title: Story, system, and QA must be chosen before slide markup begins.
slides.key_message: The HTML deck contract starts with lane fit, visual system, layout registry, and QA plan.

slides.action_title: Local image slots make visual assets inspectable instead of decorative.
slides.key_message: Every image should have a named slot, ratio, alt text, and slide ownership.
slides.visual: Local SVG asset with slot metadata.

slides.action_title: Static validation catches structure before visual QA catches taste.
slides.key_message: Validators and screenshots solve different failures and both are needed.

slides.action_title: The benchmark path runs from memo to plan to render to fix loop.
slides.key_message: A reliable HTML lane needs an explicit production sequence.

slides.action_title: Registered layouts turn visual taste into a reusable control system.
slides.key_message: The deck should show distinct slide jobs while staying inside one visual system.

slides.action_title: A benchmark deck should leave artifacts that another agent can audit.
slides.key_message: The case study includes deck plan, HTML, local image, contact sheet, screenshot, and QA report.

slides.action_title: The next quality jump is visual evidence, not more prompt instructions.
slides.key_message: Knowledge Cat reaches benchmark quality by making rendered QA part of the repository contract.
slides.visual: Closing decision page with release gate.
